Essentra ( (GB:ESNT) ) has shared an announcement.

Essentra plc has confirmed that as of 30 April 2026 its issued share capital comprised 287,420,301 ordinary shares of 25 pence each, of which 3,077,057 are held in treasury and therefore do not carry voting rights. This leaves 284,343,244 ordinary shares in circulation with voting rights, a figure that investors and other obliged parties must use when calculating whether they need to notify the market of their shareholdings under U.K. disclosure rules.

By clarifying the current number of voting shares, Essentra provides transparency for shareholders and regulators, helping ensure accurate reporting of significant holdings and changes in ownership. The update supports compliance with the Financial Conduct Authority’s Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules, and may influence how institutional and other investors assess and disclose their positions in the company.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:ESNT) stock is a Buy with a £130.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Essentra stock, see the GB:ESNT Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ESNT is a Neutral.

The score is weighed down primarily by weak profitability and deteriorating cash flow trends, alongside a very high P/E multiple. Technical indicators also lean bearish with the price below key moving averages and a negative MACD, while the balance sheet and dividend yield provide only modest support.

To see Spark’s full report on ESNT stock, click here.

More about Essentra

Persimmon ( (GB:PSN) ) has provided an announcement.

Persimmon has issued and admitted to trading 54,294 new ordinary shares of 10p each to satisfy vested awards under its 2017 Performance Share Plan for employees. The additional stock, which is fully fungible with existing shares and was admitted at various dates in April, brings the company’s total number of ordinary shares in issue and admitted to trading to 321,173,487, modestly diluting existing holders while underscoring the firm’s ongoing use of share-based compensation.

The newly admitted shares were issued under existing block admissions established in 2022, meaning no new prospectus was required for this incremental issuance. This routine capital markets operation streamlines administrative processes around employee incentives and signals continued reliance on performance-related share awards within Persimmon’s remuneration framework.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:PSN) stock is a Buy with a £1591.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Persimmon stock, see the GB:PSN Stock Forecast page.

data.title: City of London Investment Trust Confirms Total Voting Rights at 34.1 Million
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from City of London Investment Trust PLC ( (GB:CTY) ) is now available.

The City of London Investment Trust PLC has reported that, as of 30 April 2026, its issued share capital comprises ordinary shares alongside first preference, second preference and preferred ordinary stock, each with distinct voting rights structures. Ordinary shares carry one vote per 15 shares, first preference one vote per £10, second preference carries no voting rights, and preferred ordinary one vote per £20.

On this basis, the trust confirmed that the total number of voting rights in issue stands at 34,059,489, a figure that shareholders should use as the denominator when assessing whether they must disclose holdings or changes in holdings under the FCA’s Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules. The update ensures clarity for investors and regulators on the current voting capital, supporting accurate compliance with UK market transparency requirements.

Spark’s Take on CTY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CTY is a Neutral.

City of London Investment Trust PLC benefits from strong financial performance and an attractive valuation, particularly in terms of its low P/E ratio and consistent dividend policy. However, technical indicators suggest potential price weakness, and limited cash flow transparency poses some risk. Recent corporate events show management’s confidence, but the technical analysis and cash flow opacity weigh more heavily on the overall score.

To see Spark’s full report on CTY stock, click here.

More about City of London Investment Trust PLC

The City of London Investment Trust PLC is a UK-listed investment trust managed by Janus Henderson, focusing on a diversified portfolio of primarily UK equities. It offers various classes of share capital, including ordinary shares and different categories of preference and preferred stock, providing investors with income-focused exposure to the UK stock market.

Average Trading Volume: 1,061,286

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy
